seoTitle: Chisholm vs Chiswick — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Chisholm and Chiswick. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chisholm and Chiswick.
heading: Chisholm vs Chiswick — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chisholm, New South Wales and Chiswick, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chiswick has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Chisholm (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chisholm or Chiswick safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chisholm has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Chiswick scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chisholm scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Chiswick scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chisholm or Chiswick?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chiswick has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
